mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate profits and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Both GDPC1 and INDPRO show positive growth but SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term between GDPC1 and INDPRO has a coefficient < 0.1 in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS in a regression model is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer confidence and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 100k, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates strong manufacturing activity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows positive growth but SPX returns are negative in 40%+ of cases, or the 60-day rolling correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns falls below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 900k signals strong demand for residential construction, which boosts related industries and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST > 900k, or the coefficient on HOUST in a regression model is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 3%) signal a high-inflation regime that pressures corporate margins and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Both CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 3% but SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS has a coefficient > 0.1 in regression analysis.
